m_Item1.Explanation: JVM is the interpreter of byte code.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the wrong statement ?

Options.OptionText: Java virtual machine is platform dependent
JIT compiler helps to convert byte code

m_Item1.Explanation: All statements are correct. Java bytecode is platform independent which is interpreted by platform dependent JVM.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements about the Java language is true ?

Options.OptionText: Java supports only Procedural approach towards programming
Both Procedural and Object Oriented Programming are supported in Java
Java supports only Object Oriented Programming approach

m_Item1.Explanation: Java is 100% object oriented language

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is byte code in the context of Java?

Options.OptionText: The type of code generated by a Java compiler
The type of code generated by a Java Virtual Machine
It is another name for a Java source file
It is the code written within the instance methods of a class.

m_Item1.Explanation: Byte code is the intermediate code generated after compilation of java source file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the tool is used to compile java code ?
m_Item1.Explanation: javac is used for compilation of java code

m_Item1.QuestionText: The Java interpreter is used for the execution of the byte code created after source code compilation ?
m_Item1.Explanation: Byte codes are interpreted

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement is true?

Options.OptionText: Each installation for JRE hove inbuilt JDK.
Each installation for JDK have inbuilt JRE
Each installation for JVM have inbuilt JDK

m_Item1.Explanation: JDK has subcomponent as JRE which gets automatically installed when JDK is installed

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can we identify whether a compilation unit is class or interface from a .class file?

Options.OptionText: You can determine whether a .class file represents a class or an interface by examining its internal structure using tools like javap or by programmatically inspecting the bytecode.
We cannot differentiate between class and interface
The class or interface name should be postfixed with unit type

m_Item1.Explanation: Using tools like javap, you can decompile the .class file and check its details. If you run javap -v <ClassName>.class, you will see a detailed breakdown of the class file, including whether it is a class or an interface. The relevant information is found in the output's "Classfile" section, where it specifies if the file is an interface. For example, an interface will include the "interface" keyword in its definition line.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the use of interpreter?

Options.OptionText: They convert bytecode to machine language code
They read high level code and execute them
They are intermediated between JIT and JVM
It is a synonym for JIT

m_Item1.Explanation: An interpreter is a computer program that directly executes instructions written in a programming or scripting language without the need for separate compilation steps.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following option leads to the portability and security of Java?

Options.OptionText: The applet makes the Java code secure and portable

m_Item1.Explanation:
The output of the Java compiler is bytecode, which leads to the security and portability of the Java code.
It is a highly developed set of instructions that are designed to be executed by the Java runtime system known as Java Virtual Machine (JVM). The Java programs executed by the JVM that makes the code portable and secure. Because JVM prevents the code from generating its side effects. The Java code is portable, as the same byte code can run on any platform.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a Java features?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The Java language does not support pointers; some of the major reasons are listed below:
  • One of the major factors of not using pointers in Java is security concerns. Due to pointers, most of the users consider C-language very confusing and complex. This is the reason why Green Team (Java Team members) has not introduced pointers in Java.
  • Java provides an effective layer of abstraction to the developers by not using pointers in Java.
Java is dynamic, architecture-neutral, and object-oriented programming language. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which component is used to compile, debug and execute java program?
m_Item1.Explanation: JDK is core component of Java Environment and provides all the tools, executables and binaries required to compile, debug and execute a Java Program

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which component is responsible for converting byte code into machine specific code?
m_Item1.Explanation: JIT is responsible to converting byte code to the machine specific code. By using a JIT compiler, Java achieves a balance between the portability of bytecode and the performance of native machine code.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which component is responsible to run java program?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Java Runtime Environment (JRE) is responsible for running Java applications. It includes the Java Virtual Machine (JVM), along with libraries and other components necessary for executing Java programs.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which component is responsible to optimise byte code to machine code?
m_Item1.Explanation: JIT optimise byte code to machine specific language code by compiling similar byte codes at same time.This reduces overall time taken for compilation of byte code to machine specific language. .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement is true about java?
m_Item1.Explanation: Java is called ‘Platform Independent Language’ as it primarily works on the principle of ‘compile once, run everywhere’.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the below is invalid indentifier with main method?
m_Item1.Explanation: Main method cannot be private as it is invoked by JRE. Other identifier are valid with main method.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the extension of java source code files?
m_Item1.Explanation: Java files have .java extension.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the extension of compiled java classes?
m_Item1.Explanation: The compiled java files have .class extension.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is -Xms and -Xmx while starting jvm?
m_Item1.Explanation: JVM will be started with Xms amount of memory and will be able to use a maximum of Xmx amount of memory. java -Xmx2048m -Xms256m

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the below is not a Java Profiler?
m_Item1.Explanation: Profiling is the process of examining an application to locate memory or performance-related issues. When profiling a Java application, you can monitor the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and obtain data about application performance, including method timing, object allocation and garbage collection. You can use this data to locate potential areas in your code that can be optimized to improve performance. Eclipse Profiler is not a standalone Java profiler but rather a feature or plugin provided by the Eclipse IDE (Integrated Development Environment) for profiling Java applications. JProfiler is a popular java profiler. Hence JVM is not Java Profiler.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements is true?

Options.OptionText: Java supports only procedural approach towards programming
Both procedural and object oriented approach supported in java.
Only object oriented approach supported in java.

m_Item1.Explanation: Java is completely object oriented language

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which declarations of main() method are valid in order to start the execution of an application?
m_Item1.Explanation: Return type of main is void and it has to be compulsorily declared as static.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if you try to run a Java program without a main() method?
m_Item1.Explanation: The JVM looks for the main() method to start execution. If missing, it throws a NoSuchMethodError.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following best describes the role of the JVM?

Options.OptionText: Converts Java code to machine code
Executes bytecode and provides runtime environment

m_Item1.Explanation: The JVM interprets or JIT-compiles bytecode and provides memory management, garbage collection, and security.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct command to execute a compiled Java class named Hello?
m_Item1.Explanation: To run a compiled class, use java Hello (without the .class extension).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of following is platform independent?
m_Item1.Explanation: <font color="#0000ff">Bytecode created after compilation is platform independent which should be run using platform dependent JVM.</font>

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is included in the JDK but not in the JRE?
m_Item1.Explanation: The JDK includes the compiler, debugger, and other development tools, while the JRE only includes the JVM and runtime libraries.
